description_long: This is a short, cylindrical candle with a slightly irregular, organic shape, suggesting it has been partially melted and used. The candle is primarily a creamy off-white color, with subtle shading that gives it a waxy, semi-translucent appearance. The base is uneven and flared, with visible wax drips pooling around the bottom, adding to its realistic, used look. At the top center, a short, grayish wick protrudes, indicating it is ready to be lit. The surface is matte with a soft sheen, typical of paraffin or beeswax candles. There is no visible holder or base, so the candle sits directly on its own waxy bottom.
description: A creamy, partially melted cylindrical candle with a flared, wax-dripped base and a visible wick at the top. The surface is matte and slightly uneven, giving it a realistic, used appearance.
description_view_0: The candle is seen from a slight angle, highlighting the uneven, flared base with prominent wax drips and the central wick at the top.
description_view_1: This view emphasizes the candle's cylindrical body and the irregular, organic shape of the base, with the wick still visible.
description_view_2: The candle appears more upright, showing the smooth, creamy body and the subtle pooling of wax at the base.
description_view_3: The flared, melted base is most prominent here, with the wick clearly visible at the top and the candle's surface texture evident.
